What I want to talk about today is this cliche that you must have heard many, many times. If you've ever been through any seminar or self-development, you know that force inside of you, once you are at least that force, you could accomplish anything. You probably heard that cliche, right? Because it is a cliche, but here's the thing. The cliche is actually true. There actually is a force inside of you, but what they don't tell you in these seminars, because most of the people who give them were never really in the real world. They didn't make their money clawing and scratching their way to the top. So they did it by selling books and tapes in online courses. They didn't do it in the real world, right? And what they don't tell you is this amazing truth that the force inside of you is actually your own internal monologue or dialogue that you have with yourself. It's what you say to yourself. It's the voice in the back of your head that either is ruled by fear, meaning you're a fear-based person who tries to move through the world and win by playing defense, which is impossible. You can't win playing defense or it's that voice that says, I'm the I can do it. I'm capable of greatness. And you go out there and you take risk, calculate risk, you move forward and you play offense, which is the only way to win the world. You don't win playing defense. Just doesn't work. Look at any major sport out there, any world-class athlete. Yeah, defenders are important, but you don't win by playing defense. You win by scoring goals, by sinking baskets, by hitting base hits and home runs, bottom fucking line. So the question is, if that voice inside of your head is the force, that's the force, that when it's unleashed, it allows you to accomplish anything. Well, how do you do that? How do you unleash that voice? How do you get that voice to be an I can do person, to I can accomplish anything that I am this shit? I am capable of greatness. I deserve to be great. And how do you do it? I'll tell you how. It starts with selling yourself. The first person you have to sell before you ever go out into the world to try to influence anybody, try to achieve success, the first person you got to sell is you. And that's why I had you fill out that form, that wealth index, to give you an honest, accurate basis to start from. So you say, you know, let me take a good, hard look at myself. This is what I'm really good at. This is what I'm not so good at. And this is what I commit to work. Because once you make that commitment, once you make that shift, and you say, you know, I'm going to work on this, you know what, that voice will start to shift. You're smart, but your brain is smart. It knows when you got what you need to succeed at your fingertips. And it knows when you're trying to pull the wool over your own eyes. I went into the training this last few days, you know that was in Detroit, right? And what I did is I got into the nitty gritty, the granular nature of how you actually use the script. I made them read me word by word until it was almost to the point of insanity where if their inflection is a tonality or their pacing, if they didn't group words together in the route, if they didn't use the codelife, if they didn't get it exactly right, I told them to shreds. But what that does, what that does, not only does it help them actually learn the right tonality, but when they learn it, when they actually get it, and they get it very quickly, by the way, because tonality is one of those good enough aspects of the straight line, meaning you just even a little bit of it, like it clicks really easily, you can learn it quickly, right? So once it clicks for them, what happens is they say, wow, I sound different now. I'm not the same person I used to be. I just learned something new. It changes who you are. And your brain knows that, and it starts to shift that voice because you say, you know what, okay, you know what, maybe my past results don't equal my future. In other words, whatever you are in life right now, guys, here's how it really works. Here's how your psychology really works. Whatever you are right now, you have to deal with the reality that you are the sum of your past experiences in terms of beliefs. The beliefs that you currently have, as you're delusional, they're grounded in reality for the most part. Now, that reality might be incorrect. It might not serve you. It might be an easily changeable reality, but nonetheless, you come into this moment, you're listening right now with 20, 30, 40, 50 years of life behind you, and all the ups and downs, the wins, the losses, the victories, the dismal felt, all that shit, you bring into this moment. And based on that, you have a set of beliefs that tell you what's basically likely to happen in the future. You know, what are you capable of? Is it possible that for the last 35 years you've gone through life and been a $100,000 year guy, and all of a sudden you're poof, you become a million dollar guy? Your brain says, no, that's probably not likely, because that's not the person I am. That's not the skill sets I possess. That's not what I've been able to do in my life. Or you could be a million dollar guy or a $10 million dollar guy, because that's what your beliefs tell you. But here's the thing. You know how you shift those beliefs by actually working on yourself and growing your skill senses? When you change, when you learn a new skill, when you master tone out, and all of a sudden you're like, I sound good. And someone like me who says, you sound good now, or when you're out there on the phone or in person and the people you work with say, wow, you know, you've really gotten, you make some huge strides. You sound awesome. You know what your brain does? Your brain says, ah, my past really doesn't equal my future anymore, because I'm not the same person. I'm better than I was. You lead with your skill. Don't you get this? It's like the most obvious scientific thing about success that you lead with skill and success follows. You don't become successful first and then figure out how to have the skills. You must grow yourself first. You must become awesome. You must master the art of persuasion and influence and negotiation. You have to learn how to market. You have to learn how to run a business. You want to build one. You have to learn all the aspects of success and then success follows. It's not the other way around. It'll be great if it was. I guess if you're a member of the Lucky Club or you hit the lottery, I guess that can happen. But in a real world, guys, you grow yourself first. You put the work in first and get the results afterwards. How much you put in day after day after day. How much you grow yourself. How much you commit to learning. How you're able to harness that voice in the back of your head. That's what is your leading indicator to success. Show me a man or woman who's investing in themselves. I don't mean with money. I mean with learning and growth. They're doing the work and I'll show you so that next year will be ahead. Or if not that year, then that's the year after for sure. It's not magic. People want to say, what's the secret to getting? I'll tell you the secret. It's really simple. It's massive hard work and learning the skills necessary to achieve success. It's the combination of hard work, preparation and skill. A little bit of luck. But you know what happens? The harder you work, the lucker you get. Because when you work hard, you're moving through the world at a more brisk pace. And that means you find yourself in situations where opportunities pop up. Sit home on your couch and eat potato chips. How many opportunities are you going to pop up like that? Move through the world. Take massive action. Know the rules of success and watch how the opportunities come. And because now you know the rules of success, you'll seize them. You will be a person of seizing opportunity versus passing opportunity. Because that voice shifts slowly but surely day by day. This is it. This is what it's about.
